methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: defaultDesignType
properties.description: Gets and sets the default modeling type setting

properties.name: defaultWorkspace
properties.description: Gets and sets the Default workspace setting. (Model, Sculpt or Patch)

properties.name: is3DSketchingAllowed
properties.description: Gets and sets the Allow 3D sketching of lines and splines option which controls if 3D sketching is allowed or if sketching is forced to be on the x-y plane of the sketch.

properties.name: isActiveComponentVisibilityUsed
properties.description: Gets and sets the Active Component Visibility option

properties.name: isAllowReferencesDuringEditInPlace
properties.description: Gets and sets if you can create associative references while editing external components in context.

properties.name: isAutoHideSketchOnFeatureCreation
properties.description: Gets and sets if the sketch should be automatically hidden whenever a feature is created from it.

properties.name: isAutoLookAtSketch
properties.description: **RETIRED** Gets and sets if the view is re-oriented to view the newly created sketch. This property has been replaced by the isAutoLookAtSketch2 property, which provides the full capabilities.

properties.name: isAutoLookAtSketch2
properties.description: Gets and sets if the view is re-oriented to view the newly created sketch, and if it is re-oriented, if the camera uses the current camera settings or is orthographic.

properties.name: isAutoProjectEdgesOnReference
properties.description: Gets and sets if model edges should be automatically projected when creating constraints and dimensions in the active sketch when the orientation is normal to the active sketch plane.

properties.name: isAutoProjectGeometry
properties.description: Gets and Sets if geometry, not in the active sketch plane, is to be automatically projected.

properties.name: isDimensionEditedWhenCreated
properties.description: Gets and sets if dimension value is edited when the dimension is created.

properties.name: isEnableArrangeAndSimplifyTools
properties.description: Gets and sets if the Arrange, Remove Features, Remove Faces, and Replace with Primitives commands should be added to the Modify menu in the Design workspace.

properties.name: isGhostedResultBodyShown
properties.description: Gets and sets the Show ghosted result body option

properties.name: isJointPreviewAnimated
properties.description: Gets and sets the Animate joint preview option

properties.name: isSketchScaledWithFirstDimension
properties.description: Gets and sets if the sketch geometry is automatically scaled when the first dimension is added.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Returns the name of this ProductPreferences object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: FusionProductPreferences.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.FusionProductPreferences.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::FusionProductPreferences::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

fusionProductPreferences_var.defaultDesignType = propertyValue
